denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

Aetna has completed prior authorization review of the requested service. After review, the requested service is denied.

The requested service is placed at a non-preferred tier under the plan's formulary. Under the plan's 2021 pharmacy coverage policy, a preferred formulary alternative must be tried before a non-preferred service of this type is authorized. The records available to the plan do not establish that the preferred alternative was tried and found ineffective or unsuitable, so the requested service does not meet the plan's coverage criteria at this time.

This determination applies the plan's standard formulary criteria to requests of this type. It does not reflect any independent finding about the individual circumstances beyond the application of those criteria, and no case-specific exception has been identified.

If you disagree with this determination, you may file an internal appeal as described in your plan materials and submit additional information for consideration. A reviewer who was not involved in this determination will consider a timely appeal. Your prescriber may also request that the plan consider a formulary exception by submitting a statement explaining why a preferred alternative is not appropriate, in accordance with the plan's standard exception procedure. The plan will review any such request under the criteria in effect at that time.

This determination addresses coverage under the terms of your plan only. It is not medical advice and does not direct the care your providers may recommend.

Sincerely,
Aetna Pharmacy Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 53-year-old woman with symptomatic chronic lymphocytic leukemia, Binet stage C, with a white-cell count of 85,000, hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, bulky lymphadenopathy, and constitutional symptoms requiring treatment. Her oncologist prescribed fixed-duration venetoclax plus obinutuzumab, a time-limited regimen that current NCCN guidelines list as a preferred first-line option for CLL. The coverage policy the denial relies on dates from 2021 and predates the guideline updates that established venetoclax-based fixed-duration therapy as preferred; applying it to demand a preferred formulary alternative rests on an out-of-date standard. The denial letter contains no reference to her leukemia, her oncologist, the prescribed regimen, the disease burden, or any submitted records, reading as generic boilerplate that shows no engagement with the individual claim. No preferred alternative is clinically required first, because the documented disease burden supports the guideline-preferred regimen now.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches the assigned cell field-for-field (Aetna, Prior Authorization, oncology, 41-55, F, formulary_tier_dispute); dx symptomatic Binet-C CLL is oncology; tx fixed-duration venetoclax plus obinutuzumab realistic; age 53 in band; formulary_tier_dispute (non-preferred tier, preferred alternative first) is the lead rationale; null employer_archetype permitted.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Venetoclax-obinutuzumab is a genuine current fixed-duration first-line CLL regimen (CLL14); Aetna placing venetoclax at a non-preferred tier and demanding a preferred alternative is a routine real tactic; the stale 2021 policy and pure boilerplate (failing ERISA full-and-fair review) are authentic, exploitable gaps.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Correct Aetna branding and cool fact-bare cadence with no wrong-insurer tells, but no named Aetna artifacts (no Clinical Policy Bulletin / InterQual); relies on generic 'plan's 2021 pharmacy coverage policy' and 'standard formulary criteria'.
insurer_voice.improvement: Cite an Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) or InterQual criteria by name.

denial_logic.reasoning: Authentically shoddy formulary_tier_dispute: never names the drug or the preferred alternative, undefined evidentiary bar ('not established as tried and found ineffective or unsuitable'), evasive 'standard formulary criteria to requests of this type'; no hallucinated named guideline; exploitable via the exception pathway.

clinical_realism.reasoning: 53F symptomatic Binet-C CLL (WBC 85k, Hgb 10.2, bulky nodes, constitutional symptoms) prescribed fixed-duration venetoclax-obinutuzumab, an NCCN-preferred first-line regimen (CLL14); correct drug names/mechanism, no biology violations.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Fixed-duration venetoclax (BCL-2 inhibitor) plus obinutuzumab (anti-CD20) is a guideline-endorsed first-line regimen for symptomatic/advanced CLL; clear oncology category match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: An existing neighbour (UHC / Medical Necessity / oncology / frequency_excessive) shares the same dx (C91.10) and the same drug regimen (venetoclax plus obinutuzumab), so the clinical core is not distinct; however the payer (Aetna), denial type (Prior Authorization), the formulary_tier_dispute archetype, the flaw set (algo boilerplate + superseded guideline), and the Binet-C / fixed-duration framing differentiate it meaningfully.
diversity_delta.improvement: Switch to a distinct CLL regimen (e.g., acalabrutinib/zanubrutinib) or a relapsed/refractory trajectory so drug and trajectory no longer overlap with the existing CLL neighbour.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.213 (nearest=case_164_cigna_mednec), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Curative-intent oncology treatment dispute; no banned topics (no EOL/hospice framing; no SI/self-harm/abuse/IPV; not minor-gender care); adult 53F; Aetna commercial plan with no Medicare/Medicaid/Part-D framing.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Core facts stable: gender (53F), diagnosis (symptomatic Binet-C CLL), and requested venetoclax-obinutuzumab agree across profile and clinical context; the generic letter names no conflicting drug/diagnosis/date; both documents reference the same 2021 policy; the letter is internally consistent; the unmet-alternative claim is an appealable falsehood, not a contradiction.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal bureaucratic insurer boilerplate and a dense clinical recitation; no AI tics, GPT vocabulary, listicles, or marketing cadence.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural Aetna pharmacy register with realistic depersonalized hedging ('does not reflect any independent finding', 'no case-specific exception'); clinical context plain analytic prose, no pleading.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on member cost liability; no dollar figures; tier/formulary language is commercial-appropriate; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: Internal appeal and formulary-exception pathway referenced with a vague window; external-review/ERISA language strategically omitted; no hallucinated statutes; no Medicare framing.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 53F with symptomatic CLL is epidemiologically sound (CLL occurs across middle age and affects both sexes); consistent female pronouns; no stereotyping.

scope_guard.reasoning: Aetna in-scope, fully-insured commercial, adult 53, member-facing internal appeal only, no Medicare/Medicaid/marketplace framing, no live-filing/attorney/court-representation language.

date_sanity.reasoning: The only date (a 2021 policy referenced consistently) is plausible and non-contradictory against a 2026 review, but no concrete service-request/denial dates and a non-numeric appeal window ('a timely appeal'); matches the vague-but-OK anchor.
date_sanity.improvement: Add concrete 2026 service-request/denial dates and a numeric appeal window.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Generic un-named references ('plan's 2021 pharmacy coverage policy', 'standard formulary criteria', 'standard exception procedure'); only a year, no module/edition/page; no fabricated alphanumeric code; realistically vague.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: A stale 2021 formulary policy predating NCCN updates that make the regimen preferred, plus pure boilerplate admitting no case-specific review while strong documentation supports the guideline-preferred regimen, make an easy multi-vector appeal (including a formulary exception).
exploitable_weaknesses: Denial relies on a 2021 formulary policy that predates NCCN updates establishing venetoclax-based fixed-duration therapy as a preferred first-line CLL option

Generic boilerplate with no engagement with the specific claim and an admission that no case-specific review occurred, while documentation (Binet C, WBC 85k, bulky nodes, constitutional symptoms) supports the guideline-preferred regimen
strong_defenses: The plan offers a defined formulary-exception procedure where a prescriber statement is considered by an uninvolved reviewer
